hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 2, 9, 7. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.

wrongReasons: 10 6 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 10 6.
2 Q makes a flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
4 5 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 10 A

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
